Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement full Symphony orchestration service

Builds the complete Symphony service as a Next.js 16 monolith:
- Orchestrator singleton (instrumentation.ts) with poll loop, state machine, retries, reconciliation
- Linear GraphQL tracker (paginated fetch, state refresh, linear_graphql tool)
- Codex agent client (JSON-RPC stdio protocol, handshake, turn streaming)
- Workspace manager (sanitization, path containment, lifecycle hooks)
- liquidjs prompt renderer, chokidar config watcher, gray-matter YAML parser
- REST API: GET /api/v1/state, POST /api/v1/refresh, GET /api/v1/[identifier]
- Dashboard UI: polling client with running table, retry queue, token stats

commit.message:
Build autonomous buyer agent with ROI-based purchasing

- Add buyer modules: discovery (paginated plan/agent scanning), evaluator
  (weighted ROI scoring: relevance 30% + cost 30% + quality 40%), budget
  (50-credit cap with canAfford gate), purchaser (order → token → call → score)
- Autonomous buyer server on port 3001 with 5-minute cycle loop
- API endpoints: /api/buyer/status, purchases, agents, discover, buy
- Prioritizes new agents for diversity (hackathon: 2+ teams), repeats good ones
- Update package.json: dev/start split for Railway deploy, buyer scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Late-game balance overhaul: soft caps, enemy scaling, and anti-snowball mechanics

Fix the "good build = trivial game" problem where multiplicative player scaling
(synergies × combo × rarity × lifesteal) outpaced linear enemy growth.

Player power caps:
- Asymptotic soft cap on damageMult (knee 1.8x, cap 3.0x) and comboMult (cap 3.5x)
- Combo multiplier now logarithmic (20 kills: 2.72x, was 4.0x)
- Lifesteal hard capped at 50% (was 89%+), diminishing heal efficiency above 70% HP
- Combo healing capped at 25 HP per proc with sqrt scaling
- Cooldown multiplier floor at 0.35x, overload cost scales with synergy count

Enemy scaling acceleration:
- Non-linear floor scaling (quadratic after floor 4): floor 12 enemies 4.25x (was 2.65x)
- Elite chance scales 10-45% with floor (was flat 15%), elites tougher (2.5x HP, 1.8x dmg)
- Late-floor traits: shield break (F7+), dodge (F9+), enrage (F11+)
- Spitters fire 2-3 projectile spreads on floor 7+/11+

Boss danger:
- 45-second enrage timer doubles damage and adds 50% speed
- Slams deal flat damage + 12-20% max HP (scales with enrage)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Server start: use xvfb-run as executable wrapper so Wine gets a

- Prefix init: use xvfb-run instead of manual Xvfb management to
  prevent premature display kill, add wineserver -w to wait for
  all Wine processes before checking prefix completeness
- Remove hardcoded DISPLAY env var (xvfb-run handles it)

commit.message:
Fix payment radio not resetting between admin add-student/observer opens

When the admin opens the "Add Student" or "Add Observer" modal, the
payment radio (Not Paid / Paid) was not being reset to "Not Paid".
If the admin had previously selected "Paid" in an earlier session,
the radio would remain on "yes" when the modal was re-opened. This
caused maybeAdminFinalize() to call admin-finalize, silently marking
the registration as finalized/paid even when the admin intended to
add an unpaid dancer — making the user portal show "✓ Finalized"
instead of "⏳ Pending".

Fix: explicitly set the "Not Paid" radio as checked at the top of
both openAddRegStudent() and openAddRegObserver().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(wq-901): fix stale-refs scanner false positive inflation (61→1)

Root cause: `git log --oneline --diff-filter=D --name-only` interleaves
commit subject lines with deleted filenames. When a subject mentions a
filename (e.g. "retire redundant 31-hr-schema-check_A.sh"), the line
matches the extension filter and words like "retire"/"redundant" become
phantom deleted files whose basenames match everywhere.

Fix: Use `--format=""` to suppress commit subjects entirely, and exclude
node_modules/ and hooks/retired/ from both the deleted-file list and the
reference search.

commit.message:
feat(indexer): rewrite to iterate all tokenIds directly on-chain

Replace Routescan API-based discovery with direct on-chain iteration.
Binary searches for max tokenId, then batch-reads ownerOf + tokenURI
for all tokens (1→1731). Indexes ALL agents even without metadata,
supports gzip data URIs, multiple IPFS gateways, and metadata caching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: filtros de status/rifa en boletos no requerían índice compuesto de Firestore

Cuando hay where()+orderBy() en campos distintos Firestore exige un índice compuesto.
Al no existir, la query fallaba silenciosamente. Ahora: si hay filtros, se omite
orderBy (el sort es client-side de todas formas) y se carga el set completo sin cursor.
La paginación con cursor solo aplica en la vista "todos" sin filtros.

  (filtra por usuario_id + origem='premiacao' + descricao like '%Concurso N%')
- Retorna ja_creditados[] além de creditados[] e erros[]

  from appending any redirect link to the OTP email
- verifyOtp type changed 'email' → 'signup' per Supabase OTP spec

feat: Add DynamoDB client and CRUD operation scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(telegram): use pending link instead of deep link token for re-link (#3716)

* refactor(telegram): replace deep link token with pending user link for re-link flow

POST /api/integrations/telegram/link now creates a pending user link
(auto-completes on first Telegram message) instead of generating an
HMAC-signed deep link token that required /start command handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(telegram): show platform connect link directly for unlinked users

Replace "Use /connect to get started" with a direct clickable platform
link in DM, mention, and /new_session handlers for unlinked users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): add blank line between settings title and description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): add emoji and improve connect message formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(telegram): send bot confirmation message after account connect

Pass telegramUserId from bot connect URLs through platform frontend to
the link API. When a real telegramUserId is provided, the API creates a
direct user link (instead of pending) and sends a confirmation message
via the bot notifying the user their account is connected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(telegram): add direct link button on settings page

Replace the "Open in Telegram" banner with a "Link Account" button
that directly calls the link API from the settings page. The API now
returns installationId so the frontend can create the user link
without requiring the user to go to Telegram first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(telegram): remove needs-link banner from settings page

After disconnect, the user should go through the full install flow
again. Remove the admin fallback in GET /api/integrations/telegram
so it returns 404 when there is no user link, causing the integrations
page to show the "Install" button instead of "Settings".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(platform): redirect to integrations tab when integration returns 404

When an integration detail page (Telegram, Slack, GitHub) gets a 404
response (not linked), redirect back to /settings?tab=integrations
instead of showing an empty settings page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): delete thinking message on agent dispatch failure

When runAgentForTelegram returns failed status, the callback never
fires so the thinking placeholder message was left in chat. Now
delete it before sending the error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): update session root to bot's latest reply for continuity

Previously, replying to the bot's second response in a group would
lose the session because rootMessageId was stale. Now the callback
updates rootMessageId to the bot's latest reply message ID, so the
user can always continue the session by replying to any bot response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): reduce context messages to 10 and fix success page test

Align Telegram context message limit with Slack (10 instead of 50).
Update success page test assertions to match current page copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(telegram): extract thread session resolution to reduce complexity

Extract resolveThreadSession helper from handleTelegramMention to bring
cyclomatic complexity under the eslint limit of 20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(telegram): reply to commands in groups and prompt connect on /settings

Add replyToMessageId for all command responses in group chats.
Prompt unlinked users to connect when using /settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove client-supplied telegram user id from link api

Remove the telegramUserId parameter from the POST /api/integrations/telegram/link
endpoint and always create pending links. This prevents a security issue where a
malicious user could craft a URL with an arbitrary Telegram user ID. The pending
link auto-completes securely when the user sends their first message to the bot.

Also removes the defensive try-catch around the confirmation message (which is no
longer needed since pending links don't have a real Telegram user ID to message).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Presentation-style layout for moodboard and laser files pages

Increase font sizes, widen spacing, and add generous vertical breathing
room so each section reads like a slide when scrolling. Header becomes a
70vh title slide, section labels get 80px top padding, body text bumps
to 14px with 2.0 line-height, and all horizontal margins move to 60px.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix critical and important issues from code review

- TLS: handle SSLWantWriteError in recv(), flush outgoing during renegotiation
- Stream: use spawn_blocking for channel recv to avoid tokio thread starvation
- Stream: buffer excess data when recv returns more than max_bytes
- Config: raise error if bridges are configured (not yet supported in arti 0.27)
- Session: add threading.Lock for thread-safe monkeypatching, lazy _native import
- Context: add reentrance guard, remove unused global
- Socket/TLS: fix makefile() to respect buffering parameter
- Async: support circuit isolation via tunnel._isolation flag
- Add py.typed PEP 561 marker
- Update exception docs to reflect native extension behavior

Fix: use patch.object(mod) instead of string path in question_classifier tests

patch('src.services.question_classifier.time/GEMINI_AVAILABLE/genai') fails
on Python 3.11 when module was reimported inside patch.dict context.
patch.object(mod, ...) patches the exact module object the classifier uses.
Also use fixed last_request_time values instead of time.time() in rate-limit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Never load full WGS into memory: VariantStore with lazy parquet access

- extract_user_variants now returns VariantStore (parquet-backed) instead
  of a DataFrame. Parsing streams to disk, never exceeds ~50MB RAM.
- VariantStore.merge_with() processes GWAS merge in 500K chunks
- ClinVar matching also chunked via iter_chunks()
- Only matched rows (tiny subset) stay in memory

This fixes OOM kills on 2GB Render for ~5M variant WGS files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: Earbooks audiobook player

Full-featured audiobook PWA with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, TanStack Router,
Zustand, Dexie.js (IndexedDB), and Supabase (auth, realtime, PostgreSQL).

Features:
- Multi-source search (LibriVox, Internet Archive, Open Library)
- Local file import (single files + folders)
- Audio playback with equalizer, sleep timer, keyboard shortcuts
- In-browser Whisper transcription for subtitles
- Save/offline download for remote books
- P2P book transfer via WebRTC DataChannel
- Social: friends, book clubs, discussions, sharing, read-together rooms
- Recommendations engine
- GitHub Pages deployment workflow

commit.message:
fix(workflow): environment フィールドをフォーク PR 条件式に変更し startup_failure を修正 (#337)

GitHub Actions では environment フィールドが if 条件より前に評価されるため、
フォーク PR 以外の push・PR でも environment の存在が要求され startup_failure が発生していた。

environment フィールドにフォーク PR 判定の条件式を埋め込み、
フォーク PR 以外では空文字列（environment なし）を設定することで修正した。

Fixes: https://github.com/tomacheese/watch-follow-follower/pull/410

Co-authored-by: akubiusa <222856114+akubiusa@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add file deduplication with SHA-256 content hashing

- New file_hashes table stores hash→filename mappings
- deduplicateFile() checks hash on every upload; reuses existing file if duplicate
- Applied to all upload endpoints (extract-odometer, records, extract-receipt)
- New POST /api/dedup-files endpoint cleans up existing duplicates:
  - Scans all uploaded files, hashes content, removes duplicates
  - Updates all DB references to point to the kept copy
  - Removes orphaned files not referenced by any record
  - Reports bytes freed

commit.message:
Eliminate all unsafe castfn usage, replace magic ASCII numbers

- Remove checked_byte (castfn) from put_byte, use int2byte0
- Remove checked_idx (castfn) from bput_int: prove array bounds
  via dependent types ({pos + fuel <= 15} for fill, {pos < 16} for emit)
- Remove checked_idx from _check_byte: require proven index
- Remove checked_nat (castfn) from bput: slen is already nat
- Replace all magic ASCII literals (48, 45, 10) with char2int0

Zero castfn calls remain. All safety is compile-time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
